explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **structured references to specific individuals, organizations, or institutions, often with titles, roles, or affiliations** (such as named public figures with their positions, academic or governmental bodies, or formally listed groups of people).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears predominantly in news, reporting, and formal informational contexts across multiple languages (German, English, Spanish, Indonesian, etc.). Topics include politics, legal proceedings, academia, arts/culture, and sports—any domain where entities are introduced with precise identifiers (e.g., “Argentine President Mauricio Macri,” “Supreme Court... Chief Justice Francis Korkpor,” “Heriot-Watt University,” “Artistic Directors Kim Jones and Silvia Venturini Fendi”).

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: High-activation texts consistently contain complete, specific named entities coupled with descriptive roles or institutional connections. Low-activation examples either lack named entities entirely, refer to generic groups (“Mario fans,” “Native protesters”), use incomplete or vague references (“China has arrested...”), or are fragments without formal contextualization (e.g., partial sentences, standalone dates, or generic announcements).

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects linguistically structured mentions of concrete, titled entities, acting as a filter for text segments that anchor information to specific real-world actors or organizations. It signals the presence of formally identified subjects, often serving as key agents in reported events or descriptions.

results.actual_text: Keeka had accused Dhlomo of presiding over a “crisis of genocidal proportion”. He
results.plain_text: Keeka had accused Dhlomo of presiding over a “crisis of genocidal proportion”. He was referring to what he claimed to be hundreds of people who died from lack of cancer treatment. The two traded insults in written public statements.  Dhlomo accused Keeka of trying to sabotage the launch of his book My Journey to Robben Island, “by pouring scorn on my Struggle credentials.”  The book, which examined Dhlomo’s life as an activist and a political prisoner, was launched yesterday.  Keeka said, “Having chosen to launch his memoirs on Human Rights Day, one can only assume that he has done so because he regards himself as a champion of human rights.  “He (Dhlomo) is no champion of human rights. In fact, the South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) report on KwaZulu-Natal’s oncology paints a picture of a vile human rights violator.”  He accused Dhlomo of being negligent and claimed he had caused the deaths of 530 patients who died in KZN as a result of inadequate access to oncology.  He claimed there were an estimated 8000 more cancer sufferers who simply vanished, and who had presumably died because the MEC did nothing to help them when he had the opportunity.  “MEC Dhlomo is no hero and any person celebrating Human Rights Day should shun his book,” said Keeka.  “It is he who is culpable for the biggest loss of life under this failing ANC government in the last 25 years. In fact, a legal view suggests that he should be charged with murder or at the very least with culpable homicide,” he said.  Dhlomo fired back, describing Keeka as a lightweight politician with an underwhelming political career who was “paranoid and green with professional and personal envy”.  He said Keeka was abusing people’s real pain for cheap publicity evidenced by the DA’s abuse of both the Esidimeni and Marikana tragedies. He accused Keeka of trivialising cancer illness.  “To show just how hollow Keeka’s soul is, this DA lightweight, who calls himself a doctor, has never uttered a single word about the benefits of cancer screening and testing, which can lead to early detection of the disease, making it easier and cheaper to treat and cure it where possible,” said Dhlomo.

predictions.text: He (Dhlomo) is no champion of human rights. In fact, the South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) report on KwaZulu-Natal’s oncology paints a picture of a vile human rights violator.
predictions.reasoning: This section contains a formal reference to the South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC), a specific institution with a full name.

predictions.text: He claimed there were an estimated 8000 more cancer sufferers who simply vanished, and who had presumably died because the MEC did nothing to help them when he had the opportunity. “MEC Dhlomo is no hero and any person celebrating Human Rights Day should shun his book,” said Keeka.
predictions.reasoning: This section includes the titled individual 'MEC Dhlomo', a specific government official with a formal role.

He said Keeka was abusing people’s real pain for cheap publicity evidenced by the DA’s abuse of both the Esidimeni and Marikana tragedies.

results.actual_text: Buki Ponle; Executive Secretary, Nigerian Press Council, Mr. Francis Nwosu; Registrar/CEO
results.plain_text:
The Minister of Information and Culture, Lai Mohammed, has described the recent attack on The Nation Newspaper and Television Continental (TVC), during the EndSARS protest in Lagos, as an attack on free speech and democracy.  The Minister made the assertion in Lagos on Saturday when he visited the two media organizations in Lagos to commiserate with their management and staff over the attack.  He said the action of the arsonists was a premeditated attempt to silence the voice of reason and stifle press freedom.  “We believe that the attack on the Nation is not just an attack on the Vintage Press, it’s an attack on the free press, it’s an attack on democracy, because there can be no virile and robust democracy without 
a free media. “Therefore, an attack on the Nation or any media house is a direct attack on freedom of speech and also democracy and, honestly, we believe that there is no justification whatsoever for the attack,” 
Alhaji Mohammed said. He however expressed his delight that a few days after the attack, the two media houses bounced back, a development which he described as a sign of the commitment and sheer determination by the organization’s 
management and staff to continue to promote democracy in the country. “The fact that within a few days you were able to return to the stand and on air, I think it’s a big blow to the attackers, who thought they could shut out the light of press freedom,” the Minister said.  He strongly and unreservedly condemned the attack on the media houses, saying the attempt by the arsonists to stifle press freedom has failed woefully.  Alhaji Mohammed, who expressed sadness at the extent of damage to the corporate headquarter buildings and other properties of the two media houses, said there was every reason to thank God that no life was lost in the attack.  The Minister assured that the Federal Government will continue to support responsible journalism and wished the two media organizations a quick rebound in terms of properties, equipment and revenue losses. The Chairman of The Nation Newspaper, Mr. Wale Edun, and the Managing Director of TVC, Mr. Andrew Hanlon, conducted the Minister round the premises of the two media organizations, respectively.  The Minister was accompanied on the visit by the Director General, Federal Radio Corporation of Nigeria, Dr. Mansur Liman; Managing Director, News Agency of Nigeria, Mr. Buki Ponle; Executive Secretary, Nigerian Press Council, Mr. Francis Nwosu; Registrar/CEO of the Advertising Practitioners Council of Nigeria, Dr. Olalekan Fadolapo and the Acting Director General of the National Broadcasting Commission, Prof. Armstrong Idachaba.

predictions.text: The Minister was accompanied on the visit by the Director General, Federal Radio Corporation of Nigeria, Dr. Mansur Liman; Managing Director, News Agency of Nigeria, Mr. Buki Ponle; Executive Secretary, Nigerian Press Council, Mr. Francis Nwosu; Registrar/CEO of the Advertising Practitioners Council of Nigeria, Dr. Olalekan Fadolapo and the Acting Director General of the National Broadcasting Commission, Prof. Armstrong Idachaba.
predictions.reasoning: This section lists multiple high-ranking officials with their full titles and affiliations, matching the neuron's pattern of structured references to specific individuals and institutions.

results.actual_text: is ensuring providers can afford to deliver the care people need. By leveraging federal dollars as part of a 93/7 match to increase reimbursement rates for Medicaid providers
results.plain_text: Since the Affordable Care Act was signed into law nine years ago today, it has dramatically improved access to quality, affordable health care in New Hampshire — especially the protections for people with pre-existing conditions and guaranteed coverage of essential health benefits. Unfortunately, the Trump administration’s relentless attempts to dismantle the ACA threaten Granite Staters’ access to affordable health care. That’s why Senate Democrats are leading efforts at the New Hampshire State House to bolster our healthcare system in the state and put in place important backstops to this landmark law. Chief among these efforts is SB 4, which will codify into state law protections for people with pre-existing conditions and essential health benefits, including maternity and newborn care, mental health and substance use disorder services, preventive and wellness services, and more. All 14 Senate Democrats are co-sponsors on this critical bill, which goes before the full Senate for a vote next week. Almost every person or family will experience a pre-existing condition at some point in their lives. According to the Kaiser Family Foundation, New Hampshire has an estimated 201,000 non-elderly adults with pre-existing conditions — that’s how many Granite Staters could be blocked from obtaining insurance on the individual market if the ACA goes away. New Hampshire families deserve the security that comes with knowing they will have access to insurance — regardless of their medical history. New Hampshire citizens also deserve the peace of mind that their insurance will cover the care they need, when they need it. Essential health benefits go to the heart of what it means to have health insurance and what health care we, as a society, want to ensure people can access. A uniform benefit package ensures solvency and gives everyone confidence when purchasing health insurance. Beyond shoring up federal healthcare protections, we know there is more to be done in New Hampshire to strengthen and protect health care for Granite Staters. For example, many New Hampshire seniors don’t have adequate prescription drug coverage — even with Medicare Part D — and can’t afford the medicine they need. We led the effort to fill that so-called “donut hole.” SB 260 would establish a pilot program to pay out-of-pocket prescription drug costs for low-income seniors who have reached that gap in coverage. We hope Governor Sununu will support this innovative idea. A key component to improving access to care is ensuring providers can afford to deliver the care people need. By leveraging federal dollars as part of a 93/7 match to increase reimbursement rates for Medicaid providers for mental health services and substance use disorder treatment, Senate Democrats are leading the way with SB 5 to make sure behavioral health care is available where and when it’s needed in New Hampshire. Indeed, bolstering these rates was a major part of the bipartisan Medicaid expansion deal we reached last year. Unfortunately, Governor Sununu failed to implement that part of our deal, so legislation had to be filed. We hope he will sign SB 5 when it reaches his desk. Every day in our state right now, 40 to 70 people are waiting in emergency rooms and hallways for the mental health services they need. By increasing reimbursement rates and the capacity for emergency beds and transitional housing facilities, SB 11 will ensure our citizens have access to the mental health care they need, when they need it, in facilities designed to meet their needs. When people and families struggle with unexpected health crises, we know having the ability to take the time they need to heal or take care of a loved one — without fearing financial insecurity — is critical. For the first time in our state’s history, the Senate and House have passed a comprehensive paid family and medical leave insurance bill that will ensure no one is forced to choose between their job and caring for a loved one. That’s Senate Bill 1 and it goes to the governor’s desk soon; we hope he will join us to finally put in place this important tool to help New Hampshire care for our aging population, support healthy families, and combat the opioid epidemic. Every day Granite Staters share their health care stories — and struggles — with the Legislature. We owe it to the families caring for children with severe chronic illnesses, people with diabetes or heart conditions, and those in recovery to do everything we can to protect their health. Senate Democrats believe fair access to quality, affordable health care is not just a privilege for the few, but a right for all. Now we’re putting our beliefs into action to give Granite Staters the security, peace of mind, and comprehensive coverage they need, regardless of what happens at the federal level. That’s the New Hampshire way. — Democrats Dan Feltes, Concord, Cindy Rosenwald, Nashua, and Tom Sherman, Rye, serve in the New Hampshire State Senate.

predictions.reasoning: This section lists specific state senators with their names and locations, matching the pattern of structured references to individuals with political roles.

results.actual_text: ELIZABETHTON — Three groups announced Wednesday that they will "collide
results.plain_text: ELIZABETHTON — Three groups announced Wednesday that they will "collide" for a big event in downtown Elizabethton on Friday, May 14. Project Move, Create Appalachia, and Elizalliance said their collaboration will be held from 5-9 p.m. “We will utilize the after-hours atmosphere to bring you an evening to remember,” the groups said in a press release. They listed several things attendees will be able to enjoy, including a variety of street music, arts, entertainment and food trucks. “Project Move believes strongly in collaboration and understands a strong, resilient community is built by connecting its people to the resources available and providing engaging activities and options for everyone to shop, dine, play, and stay,” the release said. Peaceful Playtimes and Play It Forward will team up to bring fun activities to the Gathering Hub, located at the corner of East E and Elm streets. There will be plenty of performances, face painting and other surprises. Special guest Local Fat Boy will be at J’s Corner, where he will give the inside scoop of the best eats. Another feature will be the Tiny Art Throwdown street competition. The purpose is to use unappreciated features in streetscapes to reimagine art. For more information and a preview of happenings, visit the Project Move Facebook Page, Evenings on Elk event at https://fb.me/e/6irlOCncP.

results.actual_text: For a mall that has the word ‘America’ in its name, the Mall of
results.plain_text: For a mall that has the word ‘America’ in its name, the Mall of America sure seems to hate both freedom of speech and freedom of religion as evidenced by mall security telling a man to remove his ‘Jesus Saves’ t-shirt.  In the viral video, a man is seen wearing a bright yellow shirt that reads ‘Jesus Saves’ on the front and ‘Jesus is the only way’ on the back with the ‘Coexist’ symbol crossed out.  The mall, or at least the mall cop, didn’t appreciate the shirt and claimed that it was offending other shoppers.  “Jesus is associated with religion and it’s offending people,” the guard said. “You can take your shirt off and you can go to Macy’s and you can do your shopping — or you could leave the mall. Those are your only options right now.”  The security guard later yelled, “if you want to shop here you need to take that shirt off.”  According to a spokesperson who spoke with the Daily Mail, the man was “issued a 24-hour trespass for soliciting guests” a week prior.  The mall’s website states that it disapproves of “apparel that has obscene language, obscene gestures or racial/religious/ethnic slurs that are likely to create a disturbance.”  After the mall cop made a ridiculous scene over the Jesus Saves shirt, the man was ultimately allowed to keep his shirt on and continue shopping.  ‘Jesus Saves’ t-shirts are apparently considered ‘offensive’ in public places nowadays.  Meanwhile, the Mall of America celebrates Pride Month for over six weeks and encourages shoppers to visit stores and restaurants that “have committed to creating a culture and space where everyone is welcome.”  It sure doesn’t seem like everyone is welcome.  Follow Mark Harris on Twitter @ItIsMarkHarris

predictions.reasoning: This section names the Mall of America, a specific large shopping mall and institution.
